mcqs.question: Which memory management technique suffers from external fragmentation?
mcqs.explanation: Contiguous memory allocation suffers from external fragmentation because free memory becomes scattered in non-contiguous blocks. Paging eliminates external fragmentation by using fixed-size pages.

mcqs.question: In the FIFO page replacement algorithm, which page is replaced when a page fault occurs?

options.text: The page that was most recently used
The page that has been in memory the longest
The page that is least frequently used
The page that will not be used for the longest time

mcqs.explanation: FIFO (First-In-First-Out) replaces the page that has been in memory for the longest time, regardless of how recently it was used.

mcqs.question: What is the main advantage of paging over contiguous memory allocation?
mcqs.explanation: Paging eliminates external fragmentation by allowing non-contiguous allocation of physical memory through fixed-size pages and frames.

mcqs.question: Thrashing is caused by:
mcqs.explanation: Thrashing occurs when the system spends most of its time swapping pages in and out due to excessive page faults, indicating that processes do not have enough frames.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid page replacement algorithm?
mcqs.explanation: Priority is not a page replacement algorithm. LRU (Least Recently Used), FIFO (First-In-First-Out), and SCAN (a disk scheduling algorithm) are valid algorithms, though SCAN is for disk scheduling, not page replacement.

mcqs.question: In demand paging, when is a page loaded into memory?
mcqs.explanation: In demand paging, pages are loaded into memory only when they are referenced (on-demand), not when the process starts.

mcqs.question: Belady's anomaly is associated with which page replacement algorithm?
mcqs.explanation: Belady's anomaly occurs in the FIFO algorithm where increasing the number of frames can paradoxically increase page faults.

mcqs.question: What does the valid-invalid bit in a page table entry indicate?

options.text: Whether the page has been modified
Whether the page is in memory or not
The access rights to the page

mcqs.explanation: The valid-invalid bit indicates whether the referenced page is currently resident in physical memory (valid) or not (invalid/inactive).

mcqs.question: Copy-on-write is used to optimize:
mcqs.explanation: Copy-on-write optimizes process creation by allowing parent and child to share memory pages initially, making copies only when modifications occur.

mcqs.question: Which technique combines the benefits of both paging and segmentation?
mcqs.explanation: Segmentation with paging combines logical division (segmentation) with physical memory management (paging), providing benefits of both approaches.

mcqs.question: What is the working set of a process?

options.text: The pages a process has referenced recently
The total virtual memory of a process
The physical memory allocated to a process

mcqs.explanation: The working set is the set of pages a process has referenced within a specified time window (recent period).

mcqs.question: What type of fragmentation does paging cause?
mcqs.explanation: Paging causes internal fragmentation because pages are fixed-size and may not fully utilize the last frame allocated to a process.

mcqs.question: Which page replacement algorithm provides the minimum number of page faults?
mcqs.explanation: The Optimal algorithm provides the minimum possible page faults by replacing the page that will not be used for the longest time in the future.

mcqs.question: What happens during a page fault?

options.text: The OS loads the required page from secondary storage
The page is marked as invalid permanently

mcqs.explanation: When a page fault occurs, the OS must load the required page from secondary storage (disk) into a free frame, update the page table, and restart the instruction.

mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of swapping?

options.text: To allow more processes to run than physical memory can hold

mcqs.explanation: Swapping allows the OS to run more processes than can fit in physical memory by moving entire processes to disk temporarily.

mcqs.question: In a two-level page table, what does each entry in the first-level table point to?
mcqs.explanation: In a two-level page table, the first-level (outer) page table contains pointers to second-level page tables, which then point to physical frames.

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of the optimal page replacement algorithm?
mcqs.explanation: The optimal algorithm requires checking all frames and future references, making it O(n²) where n is the number of frames and references.

mcqs.question: What is the main disadvantage of the LRU page replacement algorithm?

options.text: It is difficult to implement in hardware
It causes more page faults than FIFO
It cannot be used with paging

mcqs.explanation: LRU is difficult to implement because it requires tracking the order of page usage, which needs special hardware support or complex software mechanisms.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an advantage of inverted page tables?

options.text: Reduced memory overhead for page tables

mcqs.explanation: Inverted page tables reduce memory overhead by maintaining one entry per physical frame rather than one per virtual page.

mcqs.question: What is the page size trade-off?

options.text: Larger pages increase internal fragmentation but reduce page table size
Page size does not affect I/O time
Larger pages reduce the number of page faults

mcqs.explanation: Larger pages reduce page table size (fewer entries needed) and I/O overhead but increase internal fragmentation. Smaller pages reduce fragmentation but increase page table size and I/O overhead.
